message:
fix(runtime,transport): explicit drop ordering for region heap slots and reordered BufferedSink fairness

runtime/region_heap.rs — When freeing a heap slot, the old code
overwrote *slot directly with the new Vacant variant, which dropped the
old HeapSlot value while still inside the borrow scope. With certain
slot payload types this could re-enter the heap (e.g. recursive Drop
calls) before the free_head/len/stats updates completed, leaving the
heap in an inconsistent intermediate state observable from a Drop
implementation.

Fix: use std::mem::replace to extract the old slot value first, complete
all heap state updates (free_head, len, bytes_live, GLOBAL_ALLOC_COUNT),
then drop the old slot explicitly via drop(old_slot) at the end. This
guarantees Drop side effects observe a fully consistent heap.

transport/sink.rs — BufferedSink::poll_send had two correctness/fairness
issues:
1. The original code unconditionally pushed to staged_symbols before
   attempting flush, even when the inner sink could accept the symbol
   directly. This caused unnecessary backlog buildup on a healthy
   downstream.
2. The flush attempt happened AFTER the symbol was staged, so a flush
   error returned Ok (to avoid duplicate delivery from caller retry)
   but also masked the real error until the next call.

Reorder: try flush first to make room. If flush returns Err, propagate
it immediately. If the buffer can accept the symbol directly after
flush (under capacity AND no existing backlog), push to buffer and
return Ready(Ok). Only stage to staged_symbols when buffer is full or
older staged work exists. The staged_symbols cap and Ok-return-on-stage
behavior are preserved from the original code (still required to avoid
duplicate delivery).

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
